content.full_text:
CHAMBERS OF
THE SUPERIOR COURT
BUTTE COUNTY COURTHOUSE
ONE COURT STREET
OROVILLE, CALIFORNIA 95965
(916) 534-4611

January 17, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Attention: Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

Re: Statement of Economic Interests
Form 721 (Government Code sec. 8700)

Gentlemen:

At the general election in November 1974 I was re-elected to a third term as a Superior Court judge. This current term of office began on January 6, 1975. It is my understanding that I am to file Form 721 (within 30 days of January 6, 1977) which apparently is intended to be an annual reporting form.

In this connection, I have the following questions which need an immediate answer:

1. Does this first anniversary statement cover the period from January 7, 1975 to January 6, 1977?
Yes _______ No _______

2. Does this first anniversary statement cover only the period from January 7, 1976 to January 6, 1977?
Yes _______ No _______

3. In addition to filing an original and one copy with the Butte County Clerk's office, is it required that another original and copy likewise be filed with the California Secretary of State's office (sec. 81005(b) Government Code)?
Yes _______ No _______

January 21, 1977

Honorable Jean Morony  
Judge of the Superior Court  
P.O. Box 918  
Chico, CA 95927

Dear Judge Morony:

This is in response to your letter of January 17, 1977. Enclosed please find a memorandum dated December 20, 1976, which explains the period covered by your Statement of Economic Interests and the due date for filing your Statement.

Pursuant to legislation enacted in the 1976 session, the Fair Political Practices Commission adopted 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18723, which sets the filing dates for Statements of Economic Interests. (copy enclosed)

You are required to file the original Statement with the clerk of the court who will make and retain a copy and forward the original to this office. No additional copies need be filed.

If you have any further questions, please contact this office.

Sincerely,

Dawn Wiser  
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
